{{Infobox concept
| name = Monarch
| introduced = Skysworn
}}

''See also: [[Monarch Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Monarchs]] are the most powerful individuals in their world, functioning as legendary kings and queens who hold immense authority. They are considered nearly mythical figures, making them extremely difficult for mortals to locate or reach. While they possess enough strength to fight [[Dreadgods]] on relatively even terms, they often face a strategic dilemma: engaging too aggressively with these entities could cause them to awaken and work together, whereas [[Monarchs]] must prioritize protecting their subjects. Ascending to the rank of Monarch is a monumental event that causes reality to tremble and the ground to shake for miles. This transition involves significant responsibilities, as [[Monarchs]] often lead great clans, rule vast territories, or act as guardians for human civilization. They possess extraordinary abilities, such as the power to tear open spatial rifts, project their will through physical forms like sand, and exert overwhelming spiritual pressure. Their influence is so great that they can communicate through a collective pulse of will and are capable of seeing far into the distance or into the destinies of others. [[Monarchs]] are often associated with immense wealth, specialized treasures, and powerful bloodlines. They may hold tournaments to showcase their students and identify future leaders. Notable examples include [[Northstrider]], the creator of [[Ghostwater]]; [[Emriss Silentborn]], who spreads peace and education; and [[Seshethkunaaz]], the [[King]] of Dragons. Despite their power, they are not infallible; they can be killed by specific weapons and must navigate complex political landscapes involving other [[Monarchs]], [[Heralds]], and the ever-present threat of the [[Dreadgods]].}}

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Monarchs]] are practically myths.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c11">''Skysworn'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|A [[Monarch]] can turn the [[Dreadgod]] aside.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c16">''Skysworn'', chapter 16</ref>}}

== Ghostwater ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Lindon]] is trapped in a [[Monarch]]'s pocket world.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c6">''Ghostwater'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|A [[Monarch]] can invent a new technique very frequently.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c13">''Ghostwater'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Monarchs]] have children who enjoy resources like Spirit [[Wells]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c19">''Ghostwater'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|A [[Monarch]] has a home with fountains and gardens.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c3">''Underlord'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The [[Monarch]] is a figure mentioned in relation to [[Mercy]]'s family history.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|A substance that strengthens the soul might be prepared in a [[Monarch]]’s pocket world.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c8">''Underlord'', chapter 8</ref>}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|During the [[arena]] competitions, the Monarch offers infallible protection to all participants. This entity's power is immense, capable of causing [[blood madra]] to splatter in a spray with a single hand. After interacting through speech with an individual, the Monarch used its power to restore [[Yerin]] to pristine condition. Ultimately, the Monarch demonstrated total authority over [[life]] and death by immediately resurrecting [[Yerin]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|A [[Monarch]]'s hand can cause [[blood madra]] to splatter like a spray.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c2">''Uncrowned'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Monarch]] provides infallible protection to competitors in the [[arena]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c13">''Uncrowned'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Monarch]] spoke to someone.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c17">''Uncrowned'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Monarch]] restored [[Yerin]] to pristine condition.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c18">''Uncrowned'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|A [[Monarch]] can immediately resurrect [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c19">''Uncrowned'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A Monarch represents a high level of power and a specific rank achieved through unique opportunities, though their true nature remains a secret to those below the [[Archlord]] stage. The birth of such a being causes [[aura]] to tremble and reality to quake, and they possess the ability to command reality directly, travel great distances with a thought, and take whatever they desire. Within the [[Ninecloud Court]], every individual in the audience hall except for [[Lindon]] held this rank, and their information is considered as valuable as a technique library. While they can transport others to opulent display halls and order major events like the start of a tournament, [[Monarchs]] are not invincible and can be killed. During these events, [[Fury]] began ascending to this stage, while another Monarch died unceremoniously.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Every person in the audience hall of the [[Ninecloud Court]] besides [[Lindon]] was a [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c2">''Wintersteel'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Monarchs]] have the ability to hear their name spoken.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3">''Wintersteel'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Northstrider]] aims to become a [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The birth of a [[Monarch]] causes reality to quake and [[aura]] to tremble.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The nature of [[Monarchs]] is a secret for anyone below [[Archlord]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Monarch]] can take anything he wants without trickery.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c7">''Wintersteel'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Monarchs]] are capable of traveling great distances at a thought.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Monarch]] expects great things from [[Mercy]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c8">''Wintersteel'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Monarchs]] can command reality directly.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Monarch factions]] do not spend advancement resources lightly.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c11">''Wintersteel'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Information from the [[Monarch]] is as valuable as any technique library.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c14">''Wintersteel'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Monarch]] is a high level of power; the tournament's start was ordered by one.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c15">''Wintersteel'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|An enemy [[Monarch]] could obtain a weapon of heavenly death.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c17">''Wintersteel'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Monarch]] can be killed.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c18">''Wintersteel'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Monarch]] is a path or rank that can be achieved through unique opportunities.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c19">''Wintersteel'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Monarch]] can transport people to opulent display halls.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c25">''Wintersteel'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Fury]] is ascending to the [[Monarch]] stage.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c26">''Wintersteel'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A [[Monarch]] died unceremoniously.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28">''Wintersteel'', chapter 28</ref>}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The title of Monarch is a rank held by [[Tiberian Arelius]], representing a significant threat. During a confrontation between the [[Wandering Titan]] and this Monarch, the [[Kazan clan]] utilized the chaos to travel. [[Lindon]] had anticipated that such a being would circle the suppression field. Furthermore, the Monarch provided a tool to the [[Sage]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|A [[Monarch]] is a threat.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2">''Bloodline'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Monarch]] is a rank or title held by [[Tiberian Arelius]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c5">''Bloodline'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Kazan clan]] traveled during a fight between the [[Wandering Titan]] and a [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17">''Bloodline'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Monarch]] is a being [[Lindon]] expected would circle the suppression field.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|The [[Sage]] was granted a tool by a [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c22">''Bloodline'', chapter 22</ref>}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Monarchs]] are immensely powerful beings capable of eavesdropping on conversations and forcing others to swear to secrecy. These entities can provide protection to [[Sages]] and [[Heralds]], though a [[Herald]]'s ultimate goal upon reaching the peak is to advance to this rank themselves. Historically, [[Monarchs]] fought on [[Naru Huan]]'s western border and created the [[Divine Treasure]], possessing the ability to deactivate clouds regardless of any [[scripts]]. Their unique [[Remnants]] can be tempered in [[Archlord soulfire]], and their armories might have once contained [[Ozmanthus]]' failed [[Penance]] prototypes. To maintain balance, [[Monarchs]] keep one another in check to prevent their numbers from growing too high.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A [[Monarch]] can provide protection to [[Sages]] and [[Heralds]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c3">''Reaper'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Eithan]] may have bartered with the [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4">''Reaper'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Monarchs]] are powerful beings that might eavesdrop on conversations.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Divine Treasure]] was created by a [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A [[Monarch]] can deactivate a cloud regardless of [[scripts]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Monarchs]] fought on [[Naru Huan]]'s western border.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c5">''Reaper'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Advancing to [[Monarch]] is the goal for a [[Herald]] who has reached the peak.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c7">''Reaper'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A [[Monarch]]'s [[Remnant]] is a type of [[Remnant]] that can be tempered in [[Archlord soulfire]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c13">''Reaper'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Forging]] a [[sword]] for a [[Monarch]] would ideally be done on a battlefield where [[Monarchs]] died.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Ozmanthus]]' failed [[Penance]] prototypes could have graced a [[Monarch]]’s armory.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c14">''Reaper'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The group fought a [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c19">''Reaper'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Eithan]] mentions punching the [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c20">''Reaper'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] wonders if the [[Monarch]] has found his way back.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c22">''Reaper'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Monarchs]] keep each other in check to prevent the number of [[Monarchs]] from becoming too high.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c25">''Reaper'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A [[Monarch]] can make people swear to keep their mouths shut.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c25" />}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A Monarch is a powerful being capable of commanding others through a sense of compulsion and compressing time into pocket worlds. While most have never entered the [[labyrinth]], they are unable to spy on individuals within its depths without explicit permission. These entities possess immense strength, such as the ability to freeze a citadel in the Wastelands using formation arrays or stabilize a spirit by combining their authority with others. [[Reigan Shen]] is identified as one such Monarch, and their power is so vast that facing the [[Silent King]] is comparable to fighting four of them simultaneously. Furthermore, a Monarch can endure significantly more stress while holding [[Akura armor]] than a [[Truegold]], and it is believed that one will eventually reach this rank if they are not killed first.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Pride]] tells [[Lindon]] he will reach [[Monarch]] eventually if no one kills him.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3">''Dreadgod'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A [[Monarch]] cannot spy on [[Lindon]] within the [[labyrinth]] without his permission.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Most [[Monarchs]] have never set foot in the [[labyrinth]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Others must be brought to the level of fighting [[Dreadgods]] and [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A [[Monarch]] used a formation array to freeze a citadel in the Wastelands.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5">''Dreadgod'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A [[Monarch]] can command others with a sense of compulsion.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6">''Dreadgod'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Fighting the [[Silent King]] is like facing four [[Monarchs]] at once.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c18">''Dreadgod'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Reigan Shen]] is referred to as a [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c19">''Dreadgod'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Monarchs]] can compress time into pocket worlds.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21">''Dreadgod'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Monarchs]] can combine their authority to stabilize a spirit.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A [[Monarch]] can hold [[Akura armor]] through more stress than a [[Truegold]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c23">''Dreadgod'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A Monarch represents a specific stage of advancement characterized by the ability to dictate how the world functions. These powerful beings can execute numerous techniques within a single minute, such as utilizing thunderbolt attacks or powering the [[Endless Sword]]. Because they cannot be exhausted or outlasted, they stand as direct opponents to [[Dreadgods]]. [[Larian]] achieved this status by drawing power from her comrades, while the will of a Monarch was once used to haul [[Lindon]] through space via a horn. Ultimately, ruling as a Monarch requires making difficult decisions, and the Book of Eternal Night is destined to end at this level.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Lindon]] tells [[Orthos]] and [[Little Blue]] they won't make it to [[Monarch]] in the time remaining.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c3">''Waybound'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A [[Monarch]] is someone who can tell the world how it works.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c8">''Waybound'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The clouds overhead were signs of a furious [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c9">''Waybound'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A [[Monarch]] can use a thunderbolt attack.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Monarchs]] and [[Dreadgods]] are opponents that cannot be exhausted or outlasted.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c10">''Waybound'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A [[Monarch]] can use many techniques in a minute.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c12">''Waybound'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Larian]] becomes a [[Monarch]] by drawing power from her comrades.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c15">''Waybound'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Mercy]] feels like she could dodge a [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Dross]] suggests [[Lindon]] should have been born to a [[Monarch]] based on the quality of [[Mercy]]'s vessel.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c20">''Waybound'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[The Phoenix]] suggests setting up [[Monarchs]] of their own.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c24">''Waybound'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[The Book of Eternal Night]] must end at [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c25">''Waybound'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|A [[Monarch]]'s will was used to haul [[Lindon]] through space via the horn.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|To rule as a [[Monarch]] requires making difficult decisions.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c26">''Waybound'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Monarch]] is a stage of advancement.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c29">''Waybound'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Endless Sword]] technique is powered by a [[Monarch]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c29" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Dross]] mentions [[Monarchs]] on [[Cradle]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c30">''Waybound'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The people in the new world were bound to powers like veiled [[Monarchs]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c36">''Waybound'', chapter 36</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Monarchs]] use spatial manipulation.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c1">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Monarchs]] command the world.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Monarchs]] are known to be capable of tearing open spatial rifts.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c6">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Monarch]] can decide to feed [[Yerin]]'s town.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c13">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 13</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|"I was aware the Titan was going to wake up, but then there was a second Dreadgod, and then Monarchs fought on my western border."<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c5" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|“Good-bye, Orthos. I’ll punch the Monarch an extra time in your name.”<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c20" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|“Flee,” Malice commanded, and Mercy felt the same compulsion to obey seize her as everyone else did.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|“Time can be compressed into pocket worlds.”<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|“You won’t make it to Monarch. Not in the time we have left.”<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c3" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle concepts]]
[[Category:Titles]]
